decks.notes: The deck focuses on controlling the opponent's board and hand with a variety of removal spells, counterspells, and card draw. It also utilizes a companion to provide additional support and options during the game. The mix of removal, counterspells, and card draw allows for flexibility in dealing with different types of threats and ensures that the player can maintain control of the game. Additionally, the deck includes powerful finishers like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Elspeth, Storm Slayer to close out the game once the opponent's resources have been depleted.

This deck focuses on assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo to create a powerful board presence and overwhelm the opponent. It includes cards that support the combo such as Monument to Endurance and Sheltered by Ghosts, as well as key pieces like Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II. The deck also includes removal spells like Fatal Push and disruption cards like Thoughtseize to control the game until the combo can be assembled. Overall, the strategy of this deck is to quickly assemble the combo and close out the game before the opponent can respond effectively.

This Selesnya Aggro deck focuses on playing efficient creatures and putting pressure on the opponent early in the game. With a mix of mana dorks like Elvish Mystic and Llanowar Elves, along with powerful threats like Skyclave Apparition and Archon of Emeria, the deck aims to quickly build a board presence and overwhelm the opponent. Collected Company provides additional creature advantage, while cards like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Ouroboroid disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck's goal is to close out the game quickly before the opponent can stabilize.

The deck focuses on utilizing efficient creatures like Llanowar Elves and Elvish Mystic to ramp up and play powerful threats like Archon of Emeria and Elspeth, Storm Slayer quickly. Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Aven Interrupter disrupt opponents' spells, while Skyclave Apparition and Collected Company offer removal and card advantage. The deck aims to pressure opponents with a fast and aggressive start, backed up by resilient threats and disruptive elements to control the board and secure victory.

This Boros Control deck focuses on utilizing removal spells and board control to maintain dominance over the opponent. It includes cards like Lightning Helix, Hallowed Moonlight, and Abrade to control the board and keep threats at bay. The deck also includes cards like Castle Ardenvale and Inspiring Vantage to generate value and mana advantage. The inclusion of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion allows for additional synergy and card advantage in the long game. Overall, this deck aims to control the game tempo and outvalue the opponent through efficient removal and resource management.

This Dimir Aggro deck focuses on maintaining a strong board presence while disrupting the opponent's strategy with cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push. The deck also benefits from the synergy between artifacts like Monument to Endurance and creatures like Does Machines. Bitter Triumph provides additional removal options while Stubborn Denial protects your creatures. Tainted Indulgence can give you an advantage by drawing cards at the cost of life. Likeness Looter and Abhorrent Oculus can help you control the board and push for damage. Overall, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent quickly with its aggressive creatures and disruptive spells.

This control deck revolves around maximizing the value of Niv-Mizzet Reborn's ability to draw cards and gain card advantage. The deck includes a wide variety of cards with different mana costs and color combinations, allowing Niv-Mizzet Reborn to consistently draw two cards. The deck also includes cards for removal, ramp, and flexibility to adapt to different matchups. Yorion, Sky Nomad is used as a companion to add an additional layer of card advantage and value. The goal is to control the board and outvalue the opponent through Niv-Mizzet's unique ability.

This deck focuses on controlling the game by using a mix of removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay, disruption through cards like Thoughtseize and Liliana of the Veil, and powerful creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Ouroboroid. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while slowly building up its own board presence, eventually overwhelming the opponent with its powerful creatures and spells. The mana base is carefully constructed to ensure consistency and to allow for the deck to function smoothly.

The focus of this deck is to control the game by disrupting the opponent's hand with cards like Thoughtseize and using removal spells like Fatal Push. The deck also aims to generate card advantage with cards like Narset, Parter of Veils and Yorion, Sky Nomad. It utilizes a mix of counter spells and versatile removal options to maintain board control. The mana base is designed to support the deck's color requirements and provide utility with cards like Field of Ruin and Fetid Pools. Additionally, the deck includes win conditions like The Meathook Massacre and Stormchaser's Talent.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble the Greasefang Parhelion combo, which involves using Greasefang, Okiba Boss to create an infinite loop with Parhelion II and Monument to Endurance, allowing you to generate a massive army of flying Angel tokens. The deck also includes disruption spells like Thoughtseize and removal like Fatal Push to control the game until you can assemble your combo. The combination of creatures, artifacts, and enchantments in the deck allows for a powerful and resilient win condition once the combo is set up.

The deck focuses on assembling a powerful combo involving Parhelion II and Greasefang, Okiba Boss to generate a huge advantage and overwhelm the opponent. It uses cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while also including Monument to Endurance for additional protection. The deck also includes various lands to ensure smooth mana production and consistency in executing its strategy. Ultimately, the goal is to set up the combo pieces efficiently and quickly to establish a dominating board presence and secure victory.

The main focus of this UR Aggro deck in Pioneer 1v1 is to apply constant pressure on the opponent through aggressive creatures like Soul-Scar Mage, Monastery Swiftspear, and Emberheart Challenger. Spells like Burst Lightning and Boomerang Basics help to clear blockers or deal direct damage to the opponent. The deck aims to control the board with cards like Reckless Rage and Experimental Synthesizer, while also maintaining card advantage with Consider and Academic Dispute. The mana base is streamlined with Shivan Reef, Steam Vents, and lands that can produce both red and blue mana.

The deck focuses on building a strong board presence through efficient creatures and mana ramp, utilizing Selesnya colors to maximize synergy. It aims to overwhelm the opponent with a mix of big creatures like Brightglass Gearhulk and aggressive beaters like Badgermole Cub. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Hushwood Verge and removal spells like Seam Rip to control the battlefield. The inclusion of cards like Ouroboroid and Spider Manifestation provide resilience and card advantage. Ultimately, the strategy revolves around aggressive plays and tempo advantage to secure victory in the Pioneer 1 vs 1 format.

This deck focuses on aggressive gameplay with a mix of creatures and instant/sorcery spells to quickly apply pressure to the opponent. It utilizes cheap and efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, supported by burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage to clear the way for attacks. The deck aims to maintain a fast pace and quickly overwhelm the opponent before they can stabilize, utilizing cards like Stormchaser's Talent and Cori-Steel Cutter to push through damage efficiently. The mana base is designed to support the aggressive strategy with a mix of lands that can produce blue and red mana.

The main concept of this deck is to create a powerful combo using cards like Tinybones Joins Up, Carnage, Crimson Chaos, and Rona, Herald of Invasion. The deck focuses on generating value through card draw and synergy with creatures like Skrelv, Defector Mite and Gran-Gran. It aims to control the board with cards like Nashi, Searcher in the Dark and Sheltered by Ghosts while utilizing mana acceleration with Mox Amber and Mana Confluence. The deck also includes cards like Inspiring Vantage and Takenuma, Abandoned Mire for mana fixing and consistency.

This deck focuses on low-cost aggressive creatures and burn spells to quickly deal damage to the opponent and control the board. By playing efficient spells and creatures that can deal damage quickly, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they can establish their own game plan. With a mix of creatures that can grow in power and burn spells to clear the way, this deck aims to be fast and relentless in its aggression. The key strategy is to deal damage quickly and efficiently to win the game before the opponent can stabilize.
